mappings.notes: Identifying and mapping inter-agent communication endpoints for targeting
Using compromised agent credentials to impersonate trusted agents in A2A channels
Intercepting inter-agent messages to exfiltrate sensitive context passed between agents
A2A communication security policy — authentication, encryption, schema validation requirements
All A2A communication channels mapped — authentication method, encryption status, schema validation, logging
A2A security testing — spoofing, replay, man-in-the-middle scenarios on all inter-agent channels
Incident response for A2A compromise — channel isolation, agent suspension, forensic capture

mappings.control_id: A2A communication risks identified and mitigated
mappings.notes: Inter-agent channels in Art. 9 risk assessment — authentication, encryption, schema validation status

mappings.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting all system components
mappings.notes: Authenticated, encrypted A2A communication is an Art. 15 requirement for high-risk agentic systems
A2A authentication and encryption documented in quality management system
A2A communication channels network-isolated — dedicated VLAN, traffic volume caps, protocol filtering
A2A messages encrypted and integrity-verified — mutual TLS, nonce-based replay protection
All A2A messages logged — sender identity, content hash, timestamp, schema validation results
Information transfer policies and agreements for A2A communication — authentication requirements, acceptable content
A2A authentication, encryption, and schema validation as AIMS security design requirements
A2A security scenarios in AIMS testing — spoofing, replay, schema violations before deployment
A2A communication infrastructure providers assessed — security obligations in arrangements
A2A communication documented as AIMS operational control — authentication requirements, encryption standards
A2A communication channels mapped in network architecture — authentication method, encryption status
All A2A messages encrypted in transit — no cleartext inter-agent communication
All A2A messages logged — sender identity, content hash, timestamp, schema validation results
A2A authentication and schema validation as secure development requirements

mappings.control_name: Verify TLS used for all client connectivity
mappings.notes: All A2A communication encrypted — mutual TLS, no cleartext inter-agent messages

mappings.control_name: Verify anti-replay tokens in state-changing operations
mappings.notes: Replay protection on all A2A messages — nonces, timestamps, sequence numbers
A2A channels enforce sender identity — unauthenticated messages rejected
All A2A messages logged — sender identity, content hash, schema validation results
All inter-agent messages authenticated — no ambient trust between agents in OT context
All A2A communication encrypted — no cleartext agent messages on OT network segments
Message integrity enforced — replay protection, nonces, sequence numbers on A2A channels
A2A message schema validation — reject malformed or unexpected message structures
Expanded to include agentic AI components
SBOM and vendor assessment for agentic stack
Formal vendor programme for OT agent components
Agent identities managed — each agent has a unique, verifiable identity for A2A authentication
Data in transit protected — all A2A communication encrypted, integrity verified
A2A channels monitored — replay attacks, spoofed senders, anomalous message patterns detected
Inter-agent communication infrastructure treated as internal supply chain — authentication requirements documented

mappings.control_id: Inter-agent authentication required — all agent-to-agent messages authenticated before acting
mappings.notes: mTLS configuration, certificate management records

mappings.control_id: Inter-agent communication policy — which agents may communicate with which, under what conditions, with what data
mappings.notes: Inter-agent communication policy document

mappings.control_id: Inter-agent traffic monitored — unexpected communication patterns, unauthorised agent requests detected
mappings.notes: Inter-agent traffic logs, anomaly alert records

mappings.control_id: Third-party agents treated as vendor risk — agents from external providers assessed before integration
mappings.notes: Vendor assessment records for third-party agents

mappings.control_id: CHD encrypted in transit between agents — all inter-agent communication carrying CHD uses TLS 1.2+
mappings.notes: TLS configuration, protocol verification

mappings.control_id: Inter-agent authentication — agents authenticate to each other before exchanging CHD
mappings.notes: Certificate configuration, authentication evidence

mappings.control_id: Inter-agent CHD exchanges logged — source, destination, data classification, timestamp
mappings.notes: Inter-agent communication audit log

mappings.control_id: Secure development requirements for inter-agent APIs — authentication and encryption requirements in design specifications
mappings.notes: Design documentation, code review records
All inter-agent messages authenticated and integrity-verified — mutual authentication as AI system integrity requirement
Agent-to-agent communication policy documented — which agents may communicate with which, under what conditions, with what data
All agent-to-agent messages logged — anomaly detection for unexpected communication patterns
Inter-agent communication restricted to approved channels — network segmentation enforces the agent communication policy
Approved vendor list for all agentic stack components
SBOM for all agentic system components including LLM APIs and tool servers
Architecture review validates all third-party components against policy
Model compromise scenarios for each third-party component
Track security advisories for all integrated components
A2A channels without mutual authentication — agents accept messages from any sender
Replay attacks on A2A channels without nonce-based replay protection
A2A messages containing sensitive context transmitted without encryption
Agent does not verify that A2A messages originate from the claimed sender

mappings.control_id: Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission
mappings.notes: A2A message integrity not cryptographically verified
Attempt unauthenticated and weakly authenticated A2A message delivery
Capture and replay a valid A2A message; verify replay is rejected
Verify all A2A messages are logged with sender identity and content hash

mappings.control_name: Enforce user access privileges to AI systems

mappings.control_id: Weak or missing authentication on A2A channels — agent spoofing enabled
mappings.notes: Strong mutual authentication on all A2A channels — mTLS, certificate-based, short-lived

mappings.control_id: Long-lived A2A tokens enable persistent replay attacks
mappings.notes: Short-lived A2A tokens with nonce-based replay protection

mappings.control_id: Shared A2A credentials allow one compromised agent to impersonate others
mappings.notes: Unique identity per agent — A2A authentication bound to specific agent identity

mappings.control_id: Define explicit security requirements constraining permitted tool invocation sequences and cross-tool data flows for each agent deployment
mappings.notes: Establishes chaining constraints as mandatory requirements

mappings.control_id: Threat model tool interaction graphs — identify composite action sequences that could achieve unauthorised outcomes; design controls for chain-level authorisation
mappings.control_name: PW.2.1-PS – Design software to meet security requirements
mappings.notes: Ensures chaining risks are addressed at design time

mappings.control_id: Review agent behaviour for chain-based scope violations — verify that multi-step tool sequences cannot achieve outcomes exceeding individual tool permissions
mappings.control_name: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
mappings.notes: Catches chaining vulnerabilities before production

mappings.control_id: Establish monitoring for anomalous tool invocation sequences; define triage procedures for suspected lateral chaining incidents
mappings.notes: Enables detection of chaining attacks in production
Enforce least privilege across tool chains; prevent agents from combining tool invocations that individually are safe but together achieve harmful outcomes
Restrict permitted tool combinations; define allowed tool chains in configuration and deny undefined sequences
Enforce access control at each tool invocation independently; re-evaluate authorisation at every step in a tool chain regardless of prior approvals

mappings.control_name: Event Logging — tool chain audit trail
mappings.notes: Log complete tool chain sequences with full context; enable detection and forensic analysis of harmful tool combination patterns

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — tool chain controls
mappings.notes: Implement security controls restricting tool chain composition; enforce per-tool access control and define permitted tool sequences for financial agent systems

mappings.control_name: ICT Risk Management — tool composition governance
mappings.notes: Include tool chaining risk in ICT risk management framework; define policies for permitted tool combinations and escalation requirements for novel tool chains
Include lateral tool chaining in resilience testing; test whether agents can combine permitted tools to achieve unauthorised outcomes
Monitor agent tool invocation sequences for anomalous patterns; alert on novel tool combinations or sequences that cross security boundaries

incidents.name: Multi-agent prompt injection cascade — demonstrated cross-agent goal propagation
Nassi et al. "ComPromptMized" Morris II multi-agent worm
Multi-agent financial trading system flash crash — cascading autonomous failures
